hypothesis: Fine-tuning SAM-EM on multi-lab experimental LPTEM datasets with varying microscope configurations, liquid chemistries, and nanoparticle material compositions will reveal that domain-specific segmentation performance degrades non-uniformly across these factors, such that performance loss under cross-lab transfer is predictable from measurable image statistics (e.g., contrast, noise texture, particle morphology variance) and can be mitigated through selective retraining on a minimal set of representative experimental conditions rather than requiring full retraining on all combinations.
